verses.text: Once Ephraim's word made men tremble, but he 'offended in Baal' and died; now they sin more, soon 'as the morning cloud.' Fading glory. Idolatry hollowed out a once-honoured tribe.
Once Ephraim's word made men tremble, but he 'offended in Baal' and died; now they sin more, soon 'as the morning cloud.' Fading glory. Idolatry hollowed out a once-honoured tribe.
Once Ephraim's word made men tremble, but he 'offended in Baal' and died; now they sin more, soon 'as the morning cloud.' Fading glory. Idolatry hollowed out a once-honoured tribe.
'I am the LORD thy God from... Egypt... thou shalt know no god but me'; but when filled, they forgot Him. Satisfied and forgetful. Plenty often breeds forgetfulness of the Giver.
'I am the LORD thy God from... Egypt... thou shalt know no god but me'; but when filled, they forgot Him. Satisfied and forgetful. Plenty often breeds forgetfulness of the Giver.
'I am the LORD thy God from... Egypt... thou shalt know no god but me'; but when filled, they forgot Him. Satisfied and forgetful. Plenty often breeds forgetfulness of the Giver.
God will meet them 'as a lion,' a leopard, and 'a bear that is bereaved.' Fierce judgment. The mercy long spurned gives way to terrible justice.
God will meet them 'as a lion,' a leopard, and 'a bear that is bereaved.' Fierce judgment. The mercy long spurned gives way to terrible justice.
'O Israel, thou hast destroyed thyself; but in me is thine help.' Where is the king they craved? He gave one 'in mine anger.' Self-ruin. Man undoes himself; salvation is found in God alone.
'O Israel, thou hast destroyed thyself; but in me is thine help.' Where is the king they craved? He gave one 'in mine anger.' Self-ruin. Man undoes himself; salvation is found in God alone.
'O Israel, thou hast destroyed thyself; but in me is thine help.' Where is the king they craved? He gave one 'in mine anger.' Self-ruin. Man undoes himself; salvation is found in God alone.
Ephraim's sin is bound up, yet God promises, 'I will ransom them from the power of the grave... O death, I will be thy plagues.' Resurrection hope. Paul echoes this triumph over death in Christ.
Ephraim's sin is bound up, yet God promises, 'I will ransom them from the power of the grave... O death, I will be thy plagues.' Resurrection hope. Paul echoes this triumph over death in Christ.
Ephraim's sin is bound up, yet God promises, 'I will ransom them from the power of the grave... O death, I will be thy plagues.' Resurrection hope. Paul echoes this triumph over death in Christ.
Though Ephraim is fruitful, 'an east wind... the wind of the LORD' shall dry his springs; Samaria shall fall. Withered. The breath of God's judgment dries up all his strength.
